events.body: Running status against the dbt project. Project parses clean — 24 models, 112 tests, last refresh 06:15 CST.

Running search for 'RevPAR', 'brand', 'region', 'OTA', 'rate parity', 'channel', 'occupancy', 'weekly'. Four upstream models surface: silver.int_reservations_spine, silver.int_channel_rate_parity, silver.int_property_kpis_daily, gold.dim_properties.
events.side_effect: tool:search matched int_reservations_spine, int_channel_rate_parity, int_property_kpis_daily, dim_properties

events.body: Running describe on each candidate. int_reservations_spine is reservation-grain with book_date, channel_code, and rate_plan_id. int_channel_rate_parity holds ota_rate_usd, direct_rate_usd, and parity_gap_pct at property-channel-day grain. int_property_kpis_daily carries revpar_usd, adr_usd, and occupancy_pct at property-day grain. dim_properties maps property_id to brand and region — the Ardmore Grand vs Heritage split lives here.
events.side_effect: tool:describe returned grain=reservation_id, property+channel+day, property+day, property_id

events.body: Running lineage. Join path is clean: property_kpis joins dim_properties on property_id, channel_rate_parity attaches via property_id and stay_date. Gap is confirmed — no model aggregates RevPAR with parity attribution to (brand, region, week). That is exactly what gold.fct_revpar_attribution_by_brand_region_week needs to be.
events.side_effect: tool:lineage validated join path · gap confirmed at brand-region-week grain

events.body: Spinning up an XS warehouse and running dbt_show on a 4-week slice filtered to Ardmore Grand brand. Sunbelt region RevPAR for the week of 2026-05-12 shows $112 vs $126 the prior week — that is an 11.1% drop. Heritage Northeast shows $143 vs $139 — a 2.9% gain. The divergence is real and the grain is viable.
events.side_effect: tool:warehouse start XS · tool:dbt_show 4-week Ardmore Grand slice · Sunbelt -11.1% vs Heritage +2.9% confirmed

events.body: Authoring models/gold/fct_revpar_attribution_by_brand_region_week.sql. Writing the config block first — table materialization, gold schema, tags revenue and ai_built.
Writing the header docblock so downstream readers know what this model is and who built it.
events.code_append:
-- gold.fct_revpar_attribution_by_brand_region_week
-- RevPAR, ADR, and OTA rate-parity gap rolled up to
-- (brand, region, week_start_date)
-- Built live by dbt-wizard sub-agents in response to CRO escalation
-- Sources: int_property_kpis_daily + int_channel_rate_parity + dim_properties

events.body: Anchoring the weekly property KPIs CTE — truncating stay_date to week and joining the property dimension to get brand and region.
events.code_append:
with weekly_kpis as (
    select
        p.property_id,
        p.brand,
        p.region,
        date_trunc('week', k.stay_date) as week_start_date,
        avg(k.revpar_usd)    as revpar_usd,
        avg(k.adr_usd)       as adr_usd,
        avg(k.occupancy_pct) as occupancy_pct,
        sum(k.rooms_sold)    as rooms_sold,
        p.rooms_available    as rooms_available
    from {{ ref('int_property_kpis_daily') }} k
    join {{ ref('dim_properties') }} p on p.property_id = k.property_id
    group by 1, 2, 3, 4, p.rooms_available
),

events.body: Adding the rate-parity CTE — averaging OTA vs direct rate gaps by brand, region, and week. Filtering to the Tue-Wed window where the parity gap was detected.
events.code_append:
parity_by_week as (
    select
        p.brand,
        p.region,
        date_trunc('week', c.stay_date) as week_start_date,
        avg(c.ota_rate_usd)      as avg_ota_rate_usd,
        avg(c.direct_rate_usd)   as avg_direct_rate_usd,
        avg(c.parity_gap_pct)    as avg_parity_gap_pct,
        count(case when dayofweek(c.stay_date) in (2, 3)
              and c.parity_gap_pct < -0.05
              then 1 end)        as tue_wed_parity_breach_days
    from {{ ref('int_channel_rate_parity') }} c
    join {{ ref('dim_properties') }} p on p.property_id = c.property_id
    group by 1, 2, 3
)

events.body: Final SELECT — one row per (brand, region, week_start_date). RevPAR, ADR, occupancy, parity gap metrics, and the count of Tue-Wed parity-breach days that drove the Sunbelt softness.
events.code_append:
select
    w.brand,
    w.region,
    w.week_start_date,
    w.revpar_usd,
    w.adr_usd,
    w.occupancy_pct,
    w.rooms_sold,
    w.rooms_available,
    coalesce(pa.avg_ota_rate_usd, 0)       as avg_ota_rate_usd,
    coalesce(pa.avg_direct_rate_usd, 0)    as avg_direct_rate_usd,
    coalesce(pa.avg_parity_gap_pct, 0)     as avg_parity_gap_pct,
    coalesce(pa.tue_wed_parity_breach_days, 0) as tue_wed_parity_breach_days
from weekly_kpis w
left join parity_by_week pa
    on  pa.brand          = w.brand
    and pa.region         = w.region
    and pa.week_start_date = w.week_start_date
order by week_start_date desc, brand, region

events.body: Writing the YAML companion. Description first, then the schema contract — every column gets a type so downstream consumers can resolve it without ambiguity.
events.code_append:
version: 2

models:
  - name: fct_revpar_attribution_by_brand_region_week
    description: |
      RevPAR, ADR, occupancy, and OTA rate-parity gap aggregated to
      (brand, region, week_start_date). Includes a count of Tue-Wed
      parity-breach days for root-cause attribution. Built by dbt-wizard
      sub-agents on 2026-05-23 in response to CRO escalation on the
      Ardmore Grand Sunbelt RevPAR drop of week 2026-05-12.
    config:
      contract:
        enforced: true
      tags: [revenue, ai_built]
      meta:
        owner: revenue_management
        built_by: dbt-wizard
        consumes: [int_property_kpis_daily, int_channel_rate_parity, dim_properties]

events.body: Adding column-level tests. Uniqueness on the composite key (brand, region, week_start_date). Not-null on revpar_usd and occupancy_pct. Accepted values on brand to catch property-dimension taxonomy drift.
events.code_append:
columns:
      - name: brand
        data_type: varchar
        tests:
          - not_null
          - accepted_values:
              values: ['Ardmore Grand', 'Heritage', 'Ardmore Select', 'Ardmore Suites']
      - name: region
        data_type: varchar
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: week_start_date
        data_type: date
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: revpar_usd
        data_type: number
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: adr_usd
        data_type: number
      - name: occupancy_pct
        data_type: number
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: avg_parity_gap_pct
        data_type: number
      - name: tue_wed_parity_breach_days
        data_type: number

    tests:
      - dbt_utils.unique_combination_of_columns:
          combination_of_columns: [brand, region, week_start_date]

events.body: Running dbt_show on the compiled model against the same 4-week slice Worker used. All column tests resolve clean. Schema contract enforces. The Sunbelt RevPAR drop is visible with 5 Tue-Wed parity-breach days and an avg_parity_gap_pct of -9.4% for Ardmore Grand Sunbelt on week 2026-05-12.
events.side_effect: tool:dbt_show compiled · tests:passed · contract:enforced · Sunbelt parity gap -9.4% confirmed · 5 breach days

events.body: Materializing gold.fct_revpar_attribution_by_brand_region_week to Iceberg on S3 via the Snowflake dbt adapter. Writing Parquet files to the gold S3 prefix. Registering with the catalog.

Running lineage one more time. Four upstreams attached. Downstream consumers — Revenue Committee dashboard, GM weekly report, rate-parity alert feed, Snowflake direct queries — can now resolve the new gold table on their next pass. The CRO's question has a production asset behind it.
events.side_effect: tool:lineage updated · downstream consumers notified · 24 models → 25 models
